location.description: PostgreSQL connection URI: postgresql://[user[:password]@]host[:port][/database]. If a database path is included, it is used as the target for pg_restore when restoring single-database dumps.

host.description: PostgreSQL server hostname (default: localhost). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: PostgreSQL server port (default: 5432). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: PostgreSQL username. Overrides the username in the location URI.

password.description: PostgreSQL password. Overrides the password in the location URI.

database.description: Target database for pg_restore when restoring a single-database dump. If omitted, the database name is inferred from the dump filename. Not used when restoring a full pg_dumpall dump (all.sql).

no_owner.description: When true, passes --no-owner to pg_restore so that ALTER OWNER commands are skipped. Useful when the roles from the source server do not exist on the target server.

single_transaction.description: When set to true, passes -1 (--single-transaction) to pg_restore so the entire restore runs inside a single transaction. Any error causes a full rollback, preventing a partially-restored database.
